texto: The man I picture in my mind is someone ordinary like me. Maybe he’s wearing glasses, maybe he’s not so handsome, but it’s how he loves me that’s extraordinary. It doesn’t matter how many people there are in a room. He knows when I’m there and he’ll find me right away, because I’m his heart and you always have to know where your heartbeat’s coming from.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Marian Tee: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “The man I picture in my mind is someone ordinary like me. Maybe he’s wearing glasses, maybe he’s …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que amor, heartbeat, true-love pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de true-love.

frase_original: The man I picture in my mind is someone ordinary like me. Maybe he’s wearing glasses, maybe he’s not so handsome, but it’s how he loves me that’s extraordinary. It doesn’t matter how many people there are in a room. He knows when I’m there and he’ll find me right away, because I’m his heart and you always have to know where your heartbeat’s coming from.

texto: I should have learned my lesson from Greek myths, really. It so doesn't pay to fall in love with a god. It’s either you get transformed – flower, bull, you name it, the gods can be that petty – or you just get…crushed.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Marian Tee sobre vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. A afirmação “I should have learned my lesson from Greek myths, really. It so doesn't pay to fall in love with …” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a amor, broken-hearted, hurt, útil em momentos de mudanca e hurt.
